The '''Black Fleet''' was the name given to the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] [[Battle group/Legends|battle group]] assigned to [[Sixth Negative Regions|Black Sword Command]].

==History==
In [[16 ABY/Legends|16 ABY]], the term came to be adopted by the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] to denote the [[Sixth Negative Regions|Black Sword Command]] [[Starship/Legends|ships]] that had disappeared in the [[Koornacht Cluster/Legends|Koornacht Cluster]] after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]. It was later discovered that at least some of these ships had been seized by the native [[Yevetha]] of the [[Duskhan League]] during the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] withdrawal in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], and they too had continued to use the name "Black Fleet" for their new navy as they augmented their prizes with new ships built to Imperial designs, and their own [[Aramadia-class thrustship|thrustships]]. It is not known how many ships were originally assigned to Black Sword Command. Intelligence recovered from the wreck of the ''[[Gnisnal]]'' in [[16 ABY/Legends|16 ABY]], however, revealed that forty-four [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ships]] had disappeared from Black Sword after Endor, consisting of five front-line [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyers]] and thirty-nine other ships undergoing construction or repair. None of these was smaller than a 900m [[Victory-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Victory''-class]] ship, and they included three [[Super Star Destroyer/Legends|Super Star Destroyers]]. To this total should also be added five [[Type II Orbital Repair Yard]]s.

The Yevetha were known to have captured the [[Black Fifteen]] shipyard, which orbited their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] at [[N'zoth]], and with it nine Star Destroyers in various states of repair and construction, and a large number of Imperial [[Prison/Legends|prisoners]], whom the Yevetha held in a camp on [[Pa'aal]]. Another yard, [[Black Nine]], was later put back into commission by the Duskhan League at [[Prildaz]], where it was destroyed in the [[Battle of ILC-905]]. The Black Fleet contained sixty or so capital ships of Imperial design by the time of the [[Yevethan Purge|Great Purge]]. More Star Destroyers were under construction at Black Nine when the shipyard was destroyed.

During the last stages of the [[Black Fleet Crisis]] in [[17 ABY/Legends|17 ABY]], the surviving Imperial captives staged a daring counter-coup. Led by [[Major/Legends|Major]] [[Sil Sorannan]], they seized control of every Imperial ship in the Duskhan League's fleet. Following their initial orders, what Sorannan dubbed the "Camp Pa'al Squadron" withdrew immediately to [[Byss/Legends|Byss]], but there they diverged. The bulk of the Imperial crews surrendered their ships to the New Republic, but four ''Victory''-class Star Destroyers joined the forces of [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Natasi Daala|Daala]] in the [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]], while two Star Destroyers and the [[Rakehell (Sorannan-class)|''EX-F'']] allied themselves with [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gilad Pellaeon/Legends|Gilad Pellaeon]] and the [[Imperial Remnant]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]]. [[Intimidator (Executor-class)|''Intimidator'']] herself disappeared into the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]], and was later found adrift as a wreck. The [[Aramadia (Super Star Destroyer)|''Aramadia'']] was apparently scuttled in the [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]] and later brought back to N'zoth, to be used as a museum.

==Known force composition==
{{Quote|According to Ayddar, the order of battle for Black Sword includes forty-four capital ships which we have not seen nor heard of since the fall of the Emperor. None smaller than a ''Victory''-class Star Destroyer. Three are Superclass vessels.|[[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Gial Ackbar]] and [[Director/Legends|Director]] [[Hiram Drayson/Legends|Hiram Drayson]]|<ref name="Before the Storm">''[[Before the Storm]]''</ref>}}
'''Super Star Destroyers'''
*[[Intimidator (Executor-class)|''Intimidator'']], renamed ''Pride of Yevetha'' as Duskhan League flagship. This ship was an [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|''Executor''-class Star Dreadnought]].
*[[Aramadia (Super Star Destroyer)|''Aramadia'']], named as a personal flagship for [[Nil Spaar]] and later used as a museum ship on N'zoth.
*One other Super Star Destroyer.

'''''Imperial''-class Star Destroyers'''
*''[[Redoubtable]]'' was badly damaged at Endor and still under repair at Black-15 when the Yevetha seized the shipyard. Later renamed ''Destiny of Yevetha''.
*At least three other vessels of this class were present at the [[battle of Doornik-319]].

'''''Victory''-class Star Destroyers'''
*[[Harridan (starship)|''Harridan'']], the guardship at N'zoth, was withdrawn to fight in the [[Battle of Notak]], and apparently never captured by the Yevetha.
*[[Valorous (Victory-class)|''Valorous'']] was captured intact by the Yevetha, renamed ''Devotion of Yevetha'', and later participated in the bombardment of [[Polneye]].

'''Other capital ships'''
*[[New Republic Intelligence Service|New Republic Intelligence]] later divulged that the smallest class of ship represented in Camp Pa'aal Squadron was the 600m [[Immobilizer 418 Interdictor-class Heavy Cruiser|Immobilizer 418]] interdictor.
*An "Interdictor Dreadnaught", the [[Imperator (Immobilizer 418 cruiser)|''Imperator'']], was responsible for the seizure of the New Republic shuttle ''[[Tampion]]''.
*The interdictor which formed part of the Yevethan fleet at the [[Battle of N'zoth]] was known in Duskhan League service as ''Splendor of Yevetha''.
*An ex-Imperial [[Corvette/Legends|corvette]] named ''[[Beauty of Yevetha]]'' had earlier been used by Nil Spaar to scout Koornacht, but was destroyed in a kamikaze run by [[T-65BR X-wing reconnaissance starfighter|Recon-X]] pilot [[Rone Taggar]] in [[16 ABY/Legends|16 ABY]].
*The engineering testbed [[Rakehell (Sorannan-class)|''EX-F'']], renamed ''Glory of Yevetha'', was a [[Sorannan-class Star Destroyer|''Sorannan''-class Star Destroyer]].
*Alongside ''Devotion'', mentioned above, ships named [[Liberty (Yevetha)|''Liberty'']] and ''[[Honor]]'' were involved at Polneye.
*A "battle cruiser" named ''[[Purity]]'' destroyed the survey ship ''[[Astrolabe]]'' at [[Doornik-1142/Legends|Doornik-1142]].
*A "cruiser" named [[Star Dream (cruiser)|''Star Dream'']] was responsible for the destruction of [[New Brigia]].
*A ship named ''[[Harramin]]'' was one of the vessels from which the Imperials initiated the [[slave circuit]]ry that allowed them to regain control of the Black Fleet during the [[Battle of N'zoth]].

'''Yevethan designs'''
*As well as sixty or so ships of Imperial design, the Duskhan League also had hundreds of [[Aramadia-class thrustship|''Aramadia''-class thrustship]], small but tough capital ships based on pre-Imperial Yevethan designs augmented with [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolasers]], [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrives]] and [[Deflector shield/Legends|deflector shields]]. Known ships of this class included Nil Spaar's ostensible flagship [[Aramadia (Aramadia-class)|''Aramadia'']], and the two guardships at Black Nine, ''[[Tholos]]'' and ''[[Rizaron]]''.

'''Shipyards'''
*[[Black Fifteen]] at N'zoth was seized intact by the Yevetha and moved to a more secure location elsewhere in the cluster.
*[[Black Nine]] was relocated by the Yevetha to Prildaz and destroyed in the [[Battle of ILC-905]].
*[[Black Eight]] and [[Black Eleven (shipyard)|Black Eleven]] were originally located at the Yevethan worlds of [[Wakiza]] and [[Zhina]].
*A fifth Type II yard, of unknown designation, was also regarded as unaccounted for by New Republic Intelligence.

'''Starfighters'''
*The Duskhan League's main starfighter was their own [[D-type fighter|delta-wing]] design.
*Star Destroyers in Yevethan service also employed some [[TIE series/Legends|TIE Series]] variants, such as the [[TIE/rc starfighter|TIE/rc scouts]] carried by ''Devotion''.
